frameworks.name: evidence accumulation across trials
frameworks.one_line: internal decision signals carry over between trials to bias subsequent choices

frameworks.name: confidence-weighted choice history bias
frameworks.one_line: past-choice influence is scaled by trial-wise confidence proxies

frameworks.name: signal-detection / perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: near-threshold motion discrimination as the behavioural substrate

core_question: Do choice history biases arise from confidence-weighted accumulation of internal decision signals, and are they tuned to the environment?
key_assumptions: internal decision signals can persist across trials in action-independent form
reaction time and accuracy index trial-wise decision confidence
observers can adapt history biases without explicit feedback
manipulations: decoupling perceptual choice from motor response trial-by-trial
stimulus-environment autocorrelation level (repeat vs alternate structure)
removal of external feedback about correctness
key_findings: history bias is explained by previous perceptual choice, not previous motor response
observers adjusted both strength and sign of history bias to environment autocorrelation
history-bias adjustment scaled with confidence proxies (accuracy and reaction time)
interpretations: choice history biases reflect adaptive, confidence-weighted accumulation of internal decision signals
the mechanism operates on action-independent decision representations rather than motor traces
